type

__mro__r

_lazyclassproperty.__get__N)
__module__
__qualname__r
unicode_seta
    A set of Unicode characters, for language-specific strings for
    ``alphas``, ``nums``, ``alphanums``, and ``printables``.
    A unicode_set is defined by a list of ranges in the Unicode character
    set, in a class attribute ``_ranges``. Ranges can be specified using
    2-tuples or a 1-tuple, such as::
        _ranges = [
            (0x0020, 0x007e),
            (0x00a0, 0x00ff),
            (0x0100,),
            ]
    Ranges are left- and right-inclusive. A 1-tuple of (x,) is treated as (x, x).
    A unicode set can also be defined using multiple inheritance of other unicode sets::
        class CJK(Chinese, Japanese, Korean):
            pass
    
_rangesc
<listcomp>?
z1unicode_set._chars_for_ranges.<locals>.<listcomp>)
extend
range
sorted
set)
retZ
_chars_for_ranges7
unicode_set._chars_for_rangesc
z+all non-whitespace characters in this range
joinr
isspacer+
printablesA
unicode_set.printablesc
z'all alphabetic characters in this ranger,
filterr.
isalphar+
alphasF
unicode_set.alphasc
z*all numeric digit characters in this ranger,
isdigitr+
numsK
unicode_set.numsc
z)all alphanumeric characters in this range)
	alphanumsP
unicode_set.alphanumsc
zVall characters in this range that are valid identifier characters, plus underscore '_'r,
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz

identcharsU
unicode_set.identcharsc
        all characters in this range that are valid identifier body characters,
        plus the digits 0-9
        r,

pyparsing_unicodezF
    A namespace class for defining common language unicode_sets.
